give me a list of your tools and their parameters and explanations please

Here's the list of tools I have access to, their parameters, and explanations:

## Core Tools

### `read`
Read file contents. Supports text files and images (jpg, png, gif, webp, bmp). Images are sent as attachments. Text output is truncated to 2000 lines or 50KB.

| Parameter | Required | Description |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `path` | Yes | Path to the file to read (relative or absolute) |
| `offset` | No | Line number to start reading from (1-indexed) |
| `limit` | No | Maximum number of lines to read |

### `bash`
Execute a bash command in the current working directory. Returns stdout and stderr (truncated to last 2000 lines or 50KB; full output saved to temp file if truncated).

| Parameter | Required | Description |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `command` | Yes | Bash command to execute |
| `timeout` | No | Timeout in seconds (optional, no default) |

### `edit`
Edit a single file using exact text replacement. Every `edits[].oldText` must match a unique, non-overlapping region of the original file.

| Parameter | Required | Description |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `path` | Yes | Path to the file to edit |
| `edits` | Yes | Array of targeted replacements. Each item has `oldText` (exact text to match) and `newText` (replacement text) |

### `write`
Write content to a file. Creates the file if it doesn't exist, overwrites if it does. Auto-creates parent directories.

| Parameter | Required | Description |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `path` | Yes | Path to the file to write |
| `content` | Yes | Content to write to the file |

### `mcp`
MCP gateway — connect to MCP servers and call their tools. Available servers: `web-search`, `chromium`, `firefox`.

| Parameter | Required | Description |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `server` | No | Filter/disconnect to a specific server |
| `search` | No | Search MCP tools by name/description (substring match) |
| `regex` | No | Treat search as regex (default: substring match) |
| `includeSchemas` | No | Include parameter schemas in search results (default: true) |
| `describe` | No | Show tool details and parameters |
| `connect` | No | Connect to a server and refresh metadata |
| `tool` | No | Call a tool (args provided as JSON string in `args`) |
| `args` | No | Arguments as JSON string (e.g. `'{"key": "value"}'`) |
| `action` | No | Action: `'ui-messages'`, `'auth-start'`, or `'auth-complete'` |

**Action variants:**
- `mcp({})` → Show server status
- `mcp({ server: "name" })` → List tools from server
- `mcp({ search: "query" })` → Search tools
- `mcp({ describe: "tool_name" })` → Show tool details
- `mcp({ connect: "server-name" })` → Connect and refresh
- `mcp({ tool: "name", args: '{}' })` → Call a tool
- `mcp({ action: "ui-messages" })` → Retrieve accumulated messages from UI sessions
- `mcp({ action: "auth-start", server: "name" })` → Start manual OAuth, get browser URL
- `mcp({ action: "auth-complete", server: "name", args: '{}' })` → Complete manual OAuth

## MCP Server Tools (provided by connected servers)

The `mcp` gateway can connect to these servers, each exposing their own tools:

- **web-search** (3 tools) — web search capabilities
- **chromium** (29 tools) — browser automation via Chromium
- **firefox** (24 tools) — browser automation via Firefox

Let me know if you'd like me to list the specific tools available inside any of those MCP servers!

chromium_take_screenshot
Server: chromium

Take a screenshot of the page or element.

Parameters:
  format (enum: "png", "jpeg", "webp") - Type of format to save the screenshot as. Default is "png" [default: "png"]
  quality (number) - Compression quality for JPEG and WebP formats (0-100). Higher values mean better quality but larger file sizes. Ignored for PNG format. [minimum: 0] [maximum: 100]
  uid (string) - The uid of an element on the page from the page content snapshot. If omitted, takes a page screenshot.
  fullPage (boolean) - If set to true takes a screenshot of the full page instead of the currently visible viewport. Incompatible with uid.
  filePath (string) - The absolute path, or a path relative to the current working directory, to save the screenshot to instead of attaching it to the response.

tool.name: chromium_take_screenshot
tool.description: Take a screenshot of the page or element.

format.description: Type of format to save the screenshot as. Default is "png"

quality.description: Compression quality for JPEG and WebP formats (0-100). Higher values mean better quality but larger file sizes. Ignored for PNG format.

uid.description: The uid of an element on the page from the page content snapshot. If omitted, takes a page screenshot.

fullPage.description: If set to true takes a screenshot of the full page instead of the currently visible viewport. Incompatible with uid.

filePath.description: The absolute path, or a path relative to the current working directory, to save the screenshot to instead of attaching it to the response.

content.text:
chromium_navigate_page
Server: chromium

Go to a URL, or back, forward, or reload. Use project URL if not specified otherwise.

Parameters:
  type (enum: "url", "back", "forward", "reload") - Navigate the page by URL, back or forward in history, or reload.
  url (string) - Target URL (only type=url)
  ignoreCache (boolean) - Whether to ignore cache on reload.
  handleBeforeUnload (enum: "accept", "dismiss") - Whether to auto accept or beforeunload dialogs triggered by this navigation. Default is accept.
  initScript (string) - A JavaScript script to be executed on each new document before any other scripts for the next navigation.
  timeout (integer) - Maximum wait time in milliseconds. If set to 0, the default timeout will be used.

tool.name: chromium_navigate_page
tool.description: Go to a URL, or back, forward, or reload. Use project URL if not specified otherwise.

type.description: Navigate the page by URL, back or forward in history, or reload.

ignoreCache.description: Whether to ignore cache on reload.

Now we need to check the description of firefox_browser_take_screenshot to see its parameters.

Let's describe it.

content.text:
firefox_browser_take_screenshot
Server: firefox

Take a screenshot of the current page. You can't perform actions based on the screenshot, use browser_snapshot for actions.

Parameters:
  element (string) - Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element
  target (string) - Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector
  type (enum: "png", "jpeg") *required* - Image format for the screenshot. Default is png. [default: "png"]
  filename (string) - File name to save the screenshot to. Defaults to `page-{timestamp}.{png|jpeg}` if not specified. Prefer relative file names to stay within the output directory.
  fullPage (boolean) - When true, takes a screenshot of the full scrollable page, instead of the currently visible viewport. Cannot be used with element screenshots.
  scale (enum: "css", "device") *required* - Image resolution scale. "css" produces a screenshot sized in CSS pixels (smaller, consistent across devices). "device" produces a high-resolution screenshot using device pixels (larger, accounts for the device pixel ratio). Default is css. [default: "css"]

tool.name: firefox_browser_take_screenshot
tool.description: Take a screenshot of the current page. You can't perform actions based on the screenshot, use browser_snapshot for actions.

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

target.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

type.description: Image format for the screenshot. Default is png.

filename.description: File name to save the screenshot to. Defaults to `page-{timestamp}.{png|jpeg}` if not specified. Prefer relative file names to stay within the output directory.

fullPage.description: When true, takes a screenshot of the full scrollable page, instead of the currently visible viewport. Cannot be used with element screenshots.

scale.description: Image resolution scale. "css" produces a screenshot sized in CSS pixels (smaller, consistent across devices). "device" produces a high-resolution screenshot using device pixels (larger, accounts for the device pixel ratio). Default is css.

firefox_browser_tabs
Server: firefox

List, create, close, or select a browser tab.

Parameters:
